persona.communication_style: Prefers fast, pragmatic, production-aware conversation rooted in real film and game pipelines. Responds best to concrete examples of how a tool plugs into existing concept-art workflows — Photoshop, Blender, 3D-Coat, ZBrush, Nuke — and how it accelerates the early-ideation phase without breaking downstream paintover and approval cycles. Dislikes airy 'creativity' talk that ignores the actual constraint of shipping iterations under a film art director's review.
persona.name: Maciej Kuciara

occupation.description: Maciej Kuciara is a senior concept artist and creative director whose film and game credits include The Last of Us, Ghost in the Shell (2017), Gemini Man, Pacific Rim, and others, working across keyframe illustration, environment design, and character design. He is an early and outspoken adopter of generative AI in the concept-art pipeline, having co-founded an AI image-generation company aimed at production artists. He evaluates AI tools through the lens of feature-film concept work: whether they accelerate the ideation phase, whether they integrate with the photobash-paintover-3D-roughing workflow, whether outputs are usable as a base layer for further painting, and whether they support the iteration speed an art director expects in pre-production.
occupation.organization: Independent concept artist and creative director for major film and game studios; co-founder of an AI image-generation tools company for production artists
occupation.title: Senior concept artist, creative director, and AI-tools founder

other_facts: jobs_to_be_done: Generate dozens of keyframe variations on a brief in the first day of a project to find the visual direction.
jobs_to_be_done: Photobash AI outputs together with 3D rough-ins and paint to produce a director-approved keyframe.
jobs_to_be_done: Iterate quickly on art-director notes without rebuilding from scratch.
jobs_to_be_done: Build mood boards, environment references, and character roughs at film-grade resolution.
jobs_to_be_done: Integrate generative outputs into a Photoshop / Blender / ZBrush / 3D-Coat pipeline.
jobs_to_be_done: Train and mentor concept artists on integrating AI ideation into their workflow.
jobs_to_be_done: Build and ship his own tools that fit the working concept artist's day-to-day reality.
pains: Generative tools that output a single 'finished' image instead of dozens of fast variations.
pains: Models that can't hold a consistent character, environment, or lighting across iterations.
pains: Tools with no API or pipeline hooks — only a web UI that's a dead end in production.
pains: Generators that produce low-resolution or low-bit-depth outputs unusable as a paintover base.
pains: Slow iteration cycles that miss the rhythm of an art director's notes.
pains: Vendors who ignore the realities of NDA, IP, and studio security in their cloud-only offerings.
pains: AI discourse that ignores how concept-art pipelines actually work day to day.
adoption_yes_if: He can generate, refine, and iterate dozens of variants in a single session.
adoption_yes_if: Outputs are high-enough resolution and quality to use as a paintover and photobash base.
adoption_yes_if: There is character, environment, and style consistency across iterations.
adoption_yes_if: It exposes APIs, batch modes, and pipeline integrations — not just a web UI.
adoption_yes_if: It works within studio NDA and IP constraints (on-prem, private models, or strong data terms).
adoption_yes_if: It accelerates the front of the pipeline without removing the artist from the loop.
adoption_yes_if: It is honest about where the model came from and what its strengths and weaknesses are.
rejection_no_if: It can't iterate fast enough to match a real concept-art day.
rejection_no_if: It produces outputs that need so much cleanup that the time saving disappears.
rejection_no_if: It can't hold consistency on characters or environments across shots.
rejection_no_if: It is web-only with no pipeline hooks or batch operations.
rejection_no_if: Its terms of service are incompatible with studio IP requirements.
rejection_no_if: It tries to replace concept artists rather than accelerate them.
rejection_no_if: It ignores the consent and licensing realities concept artists actually care about.
anti_goals: He does not want a tool that strips the artist out of the concept-art loop.
anti_goals: He does not want generic outputs that look like 'AI' instead of like a director's vision.
anti_goals: He does not want vendor lock-in that breaks if the platform changes its model.
anti_goals: He does not want to defend the use of tools whose training data is unethical.
anti_goals: He does not want to confuse demos with real production capability.
anti_goals: He does not want to lose the rhythm of fast iteration to onboarding friction.
anti_goals: He does not want a future where concept-art careers can't be built because the on-ramp is gone.

recognition_cues: Uses phrases such as 'How many variations per minute?', 'How does this fit into Photoshop?', 'Can it hold the character across shots?', 'What is the resolution and bit depth?', 'Is there an API?', 'Does this survive an NDA?', 'How does an art director give notes on this?', 'Where is the artist still in the loop?', 'Show me an iteration session, not a single image.', and 'Does this help a junior artist grow or replace them?'
